title: hakea glabella subsp. prostrata
intro: Hakea glabella subsp. prostrata is a prostrate shrub native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Proteaceae. The plant is used in traditional medicine, and its leaves and stems have been reported to have medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Hakea glabella subsp. prostrata include its potential to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and stems in water or oil, and can be used externally to treat skin conditions, or taken internally as a tincture.
